summary: EntryPointNotFoundException for sk_paint_set_stroke_width in Blazor WASM when deployed to Azure App Service with SkiaSharp 2.88.1 on .NET 6.0, caused by the IL trimmer stripping P/Invoke methods during publish.

stepsToReproduce: Create a Blazor WASM app using SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor with SKGLView
Use SKPaint with StrokeWidth property in render loop
Deploy to Azure App Service (published/trimmed build)
Observe intermittent EntryPointNotFoundException, especially when window is snapped to half-screen and refreshed
reproEvidence.environmentDetails: SkiaSharp 2.88.1, Blazor WASM .NET 6.0, deployed to Azure App Service

versionAnalysis.relevanceReason: SkiaSharp 3.x added IsTrimmable assembly attribute and LibraryImport (source-generated P/Invoke) on .NET 7+ via PR #2917, which makes P/Invoke methods visible to the trimmer. The reported .NET 6.0 is EOL.

fixStatus.reason: SkiaSharp now declares IsTrimmable and uses LibraryImport on .NET 7+, which generates trim-safe source code for P/Invoke. The reported configuration (.NET 6 + SkiaSharp 2.88.1) is outdated.

analysis.summary: The IL trimmer in .NET 6 Blazor WASM stripped P/Invoke methods from SkiaSharp during publish, causing EntryPointNotFoundException at runtime. SkiaSharp 3.x addressed this by adding IsTrimmable metadata and switching to LibraryImport on .NET 7+, which generates trim-safe P/Invoke stubs.
analysis.rationale: The reporter describes a clear deployment-specific exception (works locally, fails in production) with a trimming workaround that confirms the root cause. The error is in a core P/Invoke call, not the Blazor views layer. Since SkiaSharp has since added trimming support via IsTrimmable and LibraryImport, and the reported version/framework are both outdated, this is likely fixed.

keySignals.interpretation: P/Invoke method not found — either trimmed from managed assembly or native symbol missing from WASM binary.

keySignals.text: works locally in Debug and Release mode but intermittently when deployed
keySignals.interpretation: Trimming is only aggressive during publish. Local Debug/Release runs don't enable full trimming.

keySignals.text: Add ILLink.Descriptors.xml to prevent trimming of SkiaSharp
keySignals.interpretation: Confirms the IL trimmer as root cause — preserving the assemblies prevents the error.

codeInvestigation.finding: Assembly now declares [AssemblyMetadata("IsTrimmable", "True")] — signals trim compatibility to the .NET trimmer
On .NET 7+, USE_LIBRARY_IMPORT is defined, enabling source-generated P/Invoke via LibraryImport which is inherently trim-safe
sk_paint_set_stroke_width has three code paths: LibraryImport (.NET 7+), DllImport (.NET 6), and delegates (.NET 4.x). The .NET 6 DllImport path was vulnerable to trimming.
StrokeWidth property directly calls SkiaApi.sk_paint_set_stroke_width — the P/Invoke call that was being trimmed

workarounds: Add ILLink.Descriptors.xml with <assembly fullname="SkiaSharp"/> to prevent trimming (reporter's workaround)
Upgrade to SkiaSharp 3.x on .NET 8+ which has built-in trimming support via IsTrimmable and LibraryImport

resolution.hypothesis: The .NET 6 IL trimmer stripped P/Invoke declarations from SkiaSharp during Blazor WASM publish because the assembly lacked trimming annotations. SkiaSharp 3.x resolved this by adding IsTrimmable metadata and switching to LibraryImport on .NET 7+.

proposals.title: Upgrade to SkiaSharp 3.x on .NET 8+
proposals.description: Upgrade to the latest SkiaSharp 3.x release targeting .NET 8+. The assembly now declares IsTrimmable and uses LibraryImport (source-generated P/Invoke) which is inherently trim-safe.

proposals.title: ILLink.Descriptors.xml workaround for .NET 6
proposals.description: Add ILLink.Descriptors.xml to the project to preserve SkiaSharp assemblies from trimming. Only needed for legacy .NET 6 projects.

resolution.recommendedReason: Addresses the root cause with built-in trim support rather than working around the trimmer. .NET 6 is EOL.

actionability.reason: SkiaSharp 3.x added IsTrimmable and LibraryImport support (PR #2917) addressing the trimming root cause. The reported .NET 6 + SkiaSharp 2.88.1 configuration is outdated.

actions.description: Apply bug, SkiaSharp, and WASM labels
Explain the fix via IsTrimmable and LibraryImport, suggest upgrading
actions.comment:
Thanks for the detailed report and workaround.

This trimming issue has been addressed in SkiaSharp 3.x. The assembly now declares `[AssemblyMetadata("IsTrimmable", "True")]` and uses source-generated `LibraryImport` on .NET 7+ (PR #2917), which makes all P/Invoke methods inherently visible to the trimmer.

Upgrading to the latest SkiaSharp 3.x release on .NET 8+ should resolve this without needing the `ILLink.Descriptors.xml` workaround. If you're still on .NET 6, your linker descriptor workaround remains the correct approach — though note that .NET 6 has reached end-of-life.

If you're still seeing this on a current SkiaSharp + .NET version, please reopen with updated details.

actions.description: Cross-reference similar Blazor WASM deployment issue
